content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Defend the Feed Go E2e Reconciliation model · Theory Feed Go E2e Reconcilia…, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go E2e Reconciliation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Defend the Feed Go E2e Reconciliation model · Theory Feed Go E2e Reconcilia…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 9480 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove disposable PostgreSQL, controlled origins, process behavior, injected faults, and end-to-end reconciliation; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.

For Feed aggregation and Go Defend the Feed Go E2e Reconciliation model · Theory Feed Go E2e Reconcilia…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Matching one API response proves the ingestion pipeline.
